title: Humpback whale breaks migration record
content_html:
Humpback whale breaks migration record

Source: Janelle Weaver, Nature (online).

A lone humpback whale travelled more than 9,800 kilometres from breeding areas in Brazil to those in Madagascar, setting a record for the longest mammal migration ever documented. Humpback whales (Megaptera novaeangliae) are known to have some of the longest migration distances of all mammals, and this huge journey is about 400 kilometres farther than the previous humpback record.

The whale's journey was unusual not only for its length, but also because it travelled across almost 90 degrees of longitude from west to east. Typically, humpbacks move in a north–south direction between cold feeding areas and warm breeding grounds.

The whale, a female, was first spotted off the coast of Brazil, where researchers photographed its tail fluke and took skin samples for chromosome testing to determine the animal's sex. Two years later, a tourist on a whale-watching boat snapped a photo of the humpback near Madagascar. To match the two sightings, Stevick's team used an extensive international catalogue of photographs of the undersides of tail flukes, which have distinctive markings.

The scientists then estimated the animal's shortest possible route: an arc skirting the southern tip of South Africa and heading north-east towards Madagascar. The minimum distance is 9,800 kilometres, says Stevick, but this is likely to be an underestimate, because the whale probably took a detour to feed on krill in the Southern Ocean near Antarctica.

Most humpback-whale researchers focus their efforts on the Northern Hemisphere because the Southern Ocean near the Antarctic is a hostile environment and it is hard to get to. Scientists will probably observe more long-distance migrations in the Southern Hemisphere as satellite tracking becomes increasingly common. Daniel Palacios says that the record-breaking journey could indicate that migration patterns are shifting as populations begin to recover from near-extinction. But the reasons why the whale did not follow the usual migration routes remain a mystery.

questions.question: What TWO aspects of the whale's journey surprised researchers? (Choose TWO letters A–E)
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: aspects, journey, surprised, researchers.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "The whale’s journey was unusual not only for its length, but also because it travelled across almost 90 degrees of longitude from west to east."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc nêu rõ hành trình của con cá voi này bất thường/gây ngạc nhiên ở hai khía cạnh: chiều dài hành trình ("length" đồng nghĩa với "distance") và hướng di chuyển từ tây sang đông ("from west to east" đồng nghĩa với "direction").
Đáp án: B, C

questions.text: The passage mentions reasons why whales generally migrate. What TWO reasons are given?
questions.question: The passage mentions reasons why whales generally migrate. What TWO reasons are given?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: reasons, whales generally migrate.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "Typically, humpbacks move in a north–south direction between cold feeding areas and warm breeding grounds..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Thông thường loài cá voi này sẽ di chuyển qua lại giữa các vùng kiếm ăn ("feeding areas" tương ứng với mục đích để ăn "to eat") và các vùng sinh sản ("breeding grounds" tương ứng với mục đích sinh sản "to produce young").
Đáp án: C, E

questions.text: What TWO methods did researchers use to record the identity of the whale near Brazil?
options: They analysed part of the whale’s body.
They made notes of its behaviour.
They recorded the sounds it made.
questions.question: What TWO methods did researchers use to record the identity of the whale near Brazil?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: methods, record identity, whale near Brazil.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: "...off the coast of Brazil, where researchers photographed its tail fluke and took skin samples for chromosome testing..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tại Brazil, các nhà nghiên cứu đã dùng hai phương pháp: chụp ảnh vây đuôi của nó ("photographed" tương ứng với "took a picture") và lấy mẫu da để xét nghiệm nhiễm sắc thể ("took skin samples" tương ứng với "analysed part of the whale’s body").
Đáp án: A, E

questions.text: 4. The passage mentions places the whale may have passed close to on its journey. Which TWO places may the whale have passed?
questions.question: 4. The passage mentions places the whale may have passed close to on its journey. Which TWO places may the whale have passed?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: places, whale may have passed close to, journey.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: "...an arc skirting the southern tip of South Africa and heading north-east towards Madagascar... because the whale probably took a detour to feed on krill in the Southern Ocean near Antarctica..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi ước tính tuyến đường ngắn nhất mà con cá voi có thể đã đi, các nhà khoa học dự đoán nó đã đi qua rìa phía nam của Nam Phi (South Africa) và có khả năng đã đi đường vòng qua vùng biển Nam Cực (Antarctica) để ăn tôm krill.
Đáp án: A, E

questions.text: The passage says that more research is done in the Northern Hemisphere. Which TWO reasons are given for this?
questions.question: The passage says that more research is done in the Northern Hemisphere. Which TWO reasons are given for this?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: research, Northern Hemisphere, reasons.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: "Most humpback-whale researchers focus their efforts on the Northern Hemisphere because the Southern Ocean near the Antarctic is a hostile environment and it is hard to get to..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Các nhà khoa học tập trung nghiên cứu ở Bắc Bán cầu vì ở Nam Bán cầu môi trường rất khắc nghiệt ("hostile environment" nghĩa là Bắc Bán cầu có môi trường thân thiện hơn "friendlier surroundings") và rất khó tiếp cận ("hard to get to" nghĩa là Bắc Bán cầu dễ tiếp cận hơn "easier to reach").
Đáp án: B, D

questions.text: The passage suggests why the whale made a different journey from usual. Which TWO reasons does it suggest?
options: She did not know where she was going.
She did not want to breed.
She was looking for a new place to live.
She was recovering from an illness.
questions.question: The passage suggests why the whale made a different journey from usual. Which TWO reasons does it suggest?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: why, whale made a different journey, reasons suggested.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 8: "She could have been exploring new habitats, or simply have lost her way."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả đưa ra hai giả thuyết cho hành trình khác thường của con cá voi: có thể nó đang khám phá môi trường sống mới ("exploring new habitats" tương ứng với "looking for a new place to live") hoặc đơn giản là nó bị lạc đường ("lost her way" tương ứng với "did not know where she was going").
Đáp án: A, D

questions.text: Which TWO methods of finding out where whales migrate are mentioned in the passage?
options: omparing pictures taken in different places
questions.question: Which TWO methods of finding out where whales migrate are mentioned in the passage?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: methods, finding out where whales migrate.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 5 và Đoạn 7: "...used an extensive international catalogue of photographs... Researchers routinely compare the markings in each new photograph..." và "...as satellite tracking becomes increasingly common..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Hai phương pháp được đề cập bao gồm: so sánh các bức ảnh chụp vây đuôi ở những địa điểm khác nhau ("compare the markings in each new photograph") và theo dõi qua vệ tinh ("satellite tracking" tương ứng với việc theo dõi chuyển động từ vũ trụ "following their movements from space").
Đáp án: B, E
